findings.desc: Multiple unguarded STORAGE writes (potential storage corruption)
Modulo arithmetic (division-by-zero risk if divisor unguarded)

oracle_finding: Unchecked modulo operation can lead to out-of-bounds SSTORE
oracle_reasoning: The line `uint256_t t22 = t20 % t21;` performs an unchecked modulo operation. The result `t22` is used as an index for `MEMORY[t22] = t15;`, which can potentially write to an out-of-bounds memory location if `t20` is not properly constrained relative to `t21`.

findings.desc: Multiple unguarded STORAGE writes (potential storage corruption)
Modulo arithmetic (division-by-zero risk if divisor unguarded)

oracle_finding: Unchecked modulo operation can cause index out of bounds write
oracle_reasoning: The modulo operation `t24 = t9 % t23` can result in an index out of bounds write to STORAGE[t24] if t9 is greater than or equal to t23. There is no guard to prevent this.

findings.desc: Multiple unguarded STORAGE writes (potential storage corruption)
Modulo arithmetic (division-by-zero risk if divisor unguarded)

oracle_finding: Unchecked modulo operation can cause index overflow
oracle_reasoning: The line `uint256_t t24 = t21 % t23;` performs an unchecked modulo operation. The result `t24` is then added to `t12` to determine the SSTORE index `t25`. There is no guard to prevent `t21 % t23` from exceeding the bounds of the storage array.
